title: Mobile Launcher 1 Arrives at VAB — Artemis III Campaign Infrastructure Secured
detail: NASA's Mobile Launcher 1 completed its approximately 4-mile journey from Launch Complex 39B, arriving at the Vehicle Assembly Building at 11:40 a.m. EDT. Having departed LC-39B the previous morning at 8:11 a.m. EDT on Crawler-Transporter 2, the 380-foot-tall ML-1 tower traveled overnight to the VAB, where it will undergo additional checkouts, inspections, and repairs before Artemis III stacking operations begin. The tower sustained minor damage during Artemis II's April 1 launch and will require refurbishment before supporting the Artemis III mission — currently redesignated as a low-Earth orbit Starship HLS rendezvous and docking test targeted for launch in 2027.

sources.name: NASA Blog: Mobile Launcher Arrives at Vehicle Assembly Building
Yahoo News: With Artemis II complete, NASA rolls back mobile launcher for Artemis III

media.caption: NASA's Mobile Launcher 1 arrives at the Vehicle Assembly Building at KSC on April 17, 2026

title: Artemis II Astronauts Share Personal Reflections on Lunar Mission in NBC News Interview
detail: The four Artemis II astronauts gave a personal interview to NBC News, sharing reflections on their lunar flyby experience aboard Orion. The crew discussed the emotional and professional impact of becoming the first humans to travel to lunar distance since Apollo 17 in 1972.

media.caption: Artemis II crew shares personal reflections on historic lunar flyby in NBC News interview
